mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent indicates severe labor market deterioration and collapsing household income, which directly suppresses corporate revenue growth and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.0, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive momentum in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized strength in consumer discretionary spending and residential investment, creating a robust demand shock that drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 20-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of instances where both series show positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects tightening capacity utilization and rising marginal costs, prompting short-term risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of late-cycle observations, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes when capacity utilization remains below 78%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth accelerates corporate earnings revisions and improves equity risk premium expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the Rank IC drops below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 230 combined with rapid month-over-month acceleration triggers inflation fears that force central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns when core inflation remains stable.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle amplifies recession risks and earnings downgrade cycles, causing institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, or 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of tightening-cycle contractions.
